name: sim_gtower_topo_variant_so3_compact_vs_noncompact
divergence_log: Topology-variant rerun #1: tests that SO(3)<->U(3) pairwise coupling survives the compact vs non-compact topology distinction. Classical baseline, not yet nonclassical. Prior pairwise coupling used flat/Euclidean topology implicitly.

pytorch.reason: load_bearing: one-parameter subgroups of SO(3) computed via matrix exponential; verified periodic (compact) at t=2π; SO→U coupling survives compact topology — embedded real orthogonal matrix satisfies M†M=I for all t. Sym+(3,R) non-compact subgroup grows without bound and is not periodic, confirming topology distinction.

pyg.reason: not used in this topology-variant rerun; deferred to topology-specific tool sims

z3.reason: load_bearing: UNSAT proof — no 2×2 real orthogonal matrix can have a real eigenvalue with |λ|>1. Orthogonal matrices have eigenvalues on the unit circle; this is the algebraic signature of compact topology vs non-compact (Sym+ eigenvalues are unbounded positive reals).

cvc5.reason: not used in this topology-variant rerun; deferred to topology-specific tool sims

sympy.reason: load_bearing: symbolic eigenvalues of SO(2) rotation matrix are e^{±iθ}; |λ|=1 for both — unit circle constraint. Contrasts with Sym+(3,R) whose eigenvalues are positive reals (not on unit circle). This is the algebraic signature of compact vs non-compact topology.

clifford.reason: load_bearing: Spin(3) rotor R=cos(t/2)+sin(t/2)·e12 is compact — periodic with period 4π (double cover of SO(3) period 2π). This confirms compact topology via the Clifford algebra representation: the rotor always lies in S³ ⊂ Spin(3), bounded by definition.

geomstats.reason: load_bearing: SO(3) random sample — all elements have Frobenius norm = √3 (bounded, confirming compact topology). GL sample has unbounded norms, confirming non-compact topology of GL vs compact SO.

e3nn.reason: not used in this topology-variant rerun; deferred to topology-specific tool sims

rustworkx.reason: load_bearing: G-tower DAG annotated with compact/non-compact flags for each node. GL(3) is the unique non-compact starting point; SO/U/SU/Sp are all compact. The topology-variant claim (compact coupling survives) is encoded as reachability in the compact subgraph.
